_note: CANON: recurring characters. Seeded 2026-07 from the actual quest-givers in quests.json. The bible wants NPCs to reappear across regions and grow with the player — `appearsIn` lists where they show up (aspirationally >1 region for the recurring ones). Content references npcs by id (quests already do: giver.npc). Enforced by world_coherence.mjs (every quest giver must resolve here).

npcs.name: Goblin Elder
npcs.role: the chief's old advisor and Gorkholm's main quest-giver
npcs.arc: He feels the world stirring under the everyday — the 30% mystery thread runs through him. Grows warier and stranger as the deep things wake.

npcs.name: Sprocket the Tinker
npcs.role: inventor behind the tinkering questline
npcs.arc: Goblin invention incarnate — each contraption is bigger, louder, and more likely to explode than the last. The face of 'goblins = optimistic tinkerers.'

npcs.name: General Store Keeper
npcs.role: runs the general store; odd-job quests

npcs.name: Fishmonger
npcs.role: sells the catch; frets about the Grublake fish thieves

npcs.name: Weapon Shop Keeper
npcs.role: arms the goblins; wants a properly pointy stick

npcs.name: Grocer
npcs.role: cabbage and turnips; cabbage-for-cowards

npcs.name: Timber Row Foreman
npcs.role: runs the timber stall; complains through every Timber Glut

npcs.name: Miner Camp Boss
npcs.role: runs the northern mine camp on the edge of the old dwarf tunnels

npcs.name: Farmhand
npcs.role: works the farms; the one who swears the scarecrow moved

npcs.name: Murk the Bogling
npcs.role: a bogling snail-keeper and rust-trader in the Bog of Grub
npcs.arc: Keeper of a champion racing snail and the sacred rust — the player's way into bogling culture, and the one who only shrugs at the ancient pumps no one understands.

npcs.name: Old Judge Morel
npcs.role: moss-folk milliner and forty-year head judge of the Great Cap Festival
npcs.arc: The player's way into moss-folk culture — fashion first, then the older mushroom secrets the moss-folk keep from outsiders: the memory caps, and what no one has ever learned from them.

npcs.name: Thistle the Sprite
npcs.role: a forest-sprite caretaker of the ruin-chapel in the Old Forest Ruins
npcs.arc: The player's way into the forest-sprites — everyday tending first, then the edge of the great question they guard: who built the ruins, and who still expects them kept. Thistle knows only that the sprites have always tended, and warns against seeking the who.

npcs.name: Cairn the Pebbler
npcs.role: a pebbler older than the mine camp who measures conversation in seasons
npcs.arc: The pebblers remember what the hills remember. Ask Cairn who built the tunnels and he'll tell you — he began the answer forty years ago and is up to the second word. The player's way into pebbler patience, and the slow, slow road toward what's down there.

npcs.name: Old Taproot
npcs.role: a root-keeper whose house has been growing longer than goblin memory
npcs.arc: Architecture as a conversation with something alive. Taproot teaches taking-by-asking first, then stands the player before the oldest oak — the question even root-keepers no longer know the answer to, and have made their peace with not asking twice.

npcs.name: Granny Furrow
npcs.role: matriarch of the Main Farmlands; Best-in-Fair so many years the trophy is named after her now
npcs.arc: Harvest-as-competitive-sport made flesh — she keeps the young farmers honest by beating them. And though she'd deny it to your face, she plants one extra row every season, out toward the far field, for whatever it is that empties the offerings.

npcs.name: Old Basalt
npcs.role: toll-keeper of Troll Ridge — by day he IS the gate no goblin admits to fearing; by dusk he counts the toll
npcs.arc: The first troll a goblin can talk to instead of flee. Tolls at dusk, arguments till dawn, and somewhere in his slow stone head the next word of the four-hundred-year joke — due, he reckons, any decade now.

npcs.name: Eddy the Ferryman
npcs.role: a bogling ferryman of the Willow Riverlands; the fares are polished junk and the rates are moods
npcs.arc: Fare is whatever Eddy feels the crossing was worth — three polished spoons for a merchant, one good story for a penniless whelp. The player's way into the riverlands' pace, and a cousin-web that reaches every bogling in the marshes.

npcs.name: Knot the Logger
npcs.role: Chopper's Hollow's most reliable worker and least reliable witness
npcs.arc: Three loggers swear the tallest oak chops back, and Knot is all three of them at heart. He KNOWS WHAT HE SAW. Every camp needs its tall-tale engine — the Hollow's is the best, and the strange thing is, his stories keep almost checking out.

npcs.name: Old Pike
npcs.role: Grublake's last wreck-diver — forty years dredging the ship at the bottom of the lake
npcs.arc: Knows every plank of the wreck and not her name. His rule is the lake's rule: the first fish of the morning goes back in, and the wreck keeps her name. The player's window into what the lake keeps — and what it returns, on its own calendar.

npcs.name: Old Mallet
npcs.role: master-cutter of Grubpit Quarry; blunts every new cutter's first chisel with his own hand
npcs.arc: Sixty years in the pit — every bench, every seam, every note. The player's teacher in the cutter's way, and the keeper of the quarry's quietest fact: strike the deepest bench and something rings back, corrected, and Mallet has stopped pretending it's an echo.
